records.ety: [L. praeponderatus, p. p. of praeponderare; prae before + ponderare to weigh, fr., pondus, ponderis, a weight. See Ponder.]
records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Preponderated (?); p. pr. & vb. n. Preponderating.]

defs: To outweigh; to overpower by weight; to exceed in weight; to overbalance.
quotes: An inconsiderable weight, by distance from the center of the balance, will preponderate greater magnitudes.

defs: To overpower by stronger or moral power.
To cause to prefer; to incline; to decide.
quotes: The desire to spare Christian blood preponderates him for peace.

defs: To exceed in weight; hence, to incline or descend, as the scale of a balance; figuratively, to exceed in influence, power, etc.; hence; to incline to one side; as, the affirmative side preponderated.
quotes: That is no just balance in which the heaviest side will not preponderate.
